items.title: Tidal
items.medium: Acrylic Paint and Spray Paint on Raw Linen
items.technique: Acrylic Paint and Spray Paint on Raw Linen
items.dimensions: 300cm x 1000cm (7 separate panels arranged in an arc)
items.colorPalette: Copper, gold, cream, blue-black, burnt sienna, raw umber
items.journalExcerpt: The commission asked for lunar phases. I gave them a breathing cycle. Seven panels curving around a rotunda—the architecture itself became the ribcage. Each crescent is a held breath; the central sphere exhales. Sumi ink on Anhui paper at this scale required the pendulum rig to swing for hours. I watched the lines accumulate like tides. The oculus above mirrors the central moon. I didn't plan that. The building knew.
items.neonReading: Acrylic and spray paint on raw linen, 300cm × 1000cm across seven panels arranged in an arc within the Swire rotunda. Site-specific meditation on containment and release. The architecture itself becomes the ribcage. Each crescent is a held breath; the central sphere exhales. The pendulum rig swung for hours, lines accumulating like tides. Burnt sienna, raw umber, oxidized copper flanking panels are the lungs contracting. The central orb is the pause between inhale and exhale: the moment where nothing moves and everything is possible. Moiré interference patterns suggest harmonic resonance, sound made visible, time made tactile. The ceiling oculus mirrors the central moon. Not planned. The building knew. Monument-scale work pursued through fragile means. That tension is structural, not incidental.
items.curatorialHook: A site-specific respiratory system where gravity as a collaborator ground the sky and completes the architectural circuit of the rotunda.

items.title: Covenant I: Storm-body
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: Panel I: Collapse and turbulence. The monolithic presence has shattered into horizontal chaos. This is the storm-body that must be organized.
items.neonReading: Panel I. The first stage of the law: acknowledging chaos. Horizontal flows without coherence, lower masses weighted and collapsed. The monolithic presence has shattered into turbulence. This is the shadow before it becomes architecture, the raw material of disorder that the subsequent panels will organize. Not failure. Inventory.
items.curatorialHook: The first stage of the law: a visual acknowledgment of horizontal chaos and collapsed masses before they are organized into ritual architecture.

items.title: Covenant II: Vessel-body
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: Panel II: Structure. Organizes around a vertical axis with rib-like void chambers. Pressure energy holds what the storm scattered.
items.neonReading: Panel II. The second stage: containment. A vertical axis appears where Storm-body had none, organizing the chaos into load-bearing structure with rib-like void chambers. Pressure energy holds what the storm scattered. This is the crucible at work, weight becoming architecture, disorder becoming vessel. The transition from entropy to order is not gradual. It is structural. Something clicked into place.
items.curatorialHook: The appearance of a vertical axis organizes chaos into a load-bearing structure, literalizing the transition from weight to architectural containment.

items.title: Covenant III: Threshold-body
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: Panel III: Emergence. Upward trajectories and a clear threshold. Release energy lifts and opens the void as a luminous portal.
items.neonReading: Panel III. The third stage: release. Marks thin and lift, opening a coherent void. Upward trajectories and a clear threshold. This is negative light: luminosity created through emptiness organized into a gateway. The Covenant is sealed here. Chaos became vessel became door.
items.curatorialHook: Luminosity is created through emptiness, sealing the covenant by opening the negative space into a coherent portal of release.

items.title: Thr3e (standing Bruise)
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.colorPalette: Black, Gray, Tar-like matte monolith mass with central bruise-light wound
items.journalExcerpt: This painting is the Anchor of the 2025 series. While the rectangular works (100cm × 200cm) are the Explorations, this square work is the Definition. It proves that the New Era is not just about size or movement, but about density and presence.
items.neonReading: The square format is a trap. Where the rectangular Big Bang works allow energy to bleed outward along the longer axis, this format reflects force back into the interior, building internal pressure until the image becomes a sealed vessel. Thr3e compresses eruptive field-logic into crucible-logic. The bruise-light at center is not transcendent: it is persistent, the survival of consciousness within overwhelming shadow. This is the moment the practice shifts from meteorology (storms that pass) to geology (bedrock that persists). The Anchor specimen. The Definition.
items.curatorialHook: The definitive specimen of the New Era, compressing the eruptive Big Bang field into a square pressure vessel to document the survival of consciousness under pressure.

items.title: Big Bang: Smoke Field
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Breath—feathering, halos, and negative space representing saved territory where ink approached but did not consume. Essential to preventing the image from collapsing into pure darkness.
items.neonReading: Atmospheric-dispersed typology. Cloud-masses and breath-space dominate the field, the Breath register fully activated: graduated density aureoles reading as the work's respiratory system made visible. The quietest specimen in the series, holding tension between presence and dissolution. The ink has not struck the paper so much as settled onto it, the way fog settles into a valley. Gravity is present but unhurried. Time is slow here.
items.curatorialHook: The series’ most quiet tension between presence and dissolution, where graduated density aureoles activate a fully realized respiratory architecture.

items.title: Big Bang: Geological Core II
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: The ink pools, settles, responds to earth pull. Paper becomes a basin catching what falls. This is not possible with vertical easel work.
items.neonReading: Maximum compression at center with explosive periphery. Where Geological Core documented formation, this work documents pressure: strata compressed into near-singularity, the Flesh register generating turbulence, density, and internal heat. Ink as tectonic force. The pools settled, responded to earth-pull, and the paper became a basin catching what falls. This is not possible with vertical easel work. The floor is the collaborator.
items.curatorialHook: Maximum compression at the center transforms the paper into a basin, catching the fallout of ink as a tectonic force responding to the earth’s pull.

items.title: Big Bang: Cloud Mass
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: These works function as temples dedicated to the act of creation rather than documents of it. Temple is not photograph of worship but site where worship occurred and can occur again.
items.neonReading: Atmospheric-dispersed, cousin to Smoke Field. Cloud-masses dominate, the work hovering in the unstable zone between representation (it looks like clouds) and abstraction (it is ink behavior). Field logic at its most elemental: pure atmosphere, pure breath, minimal structure. These works function as temples dedicated to the act of creation rather than documents of it. Temple is not photograph of worship but site where worship occurred and can occur again.
items.curatorialHook: Pure atmospheric breath that hovers between representational weather systems and elemental field logic, marking the series’ most minimal structural state.

items.title: Big Bang: Shadow Self
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: Most bounded, creature-like form in series. If Shadow were to have shape, it might look like this—entity floating against void, both part of self and separate from it.
items.neonReading: The outlier. Bounded-figural rather than field-based, this work reads as organism against void: a confrontation with the Shadow Self in Jungian terms. Warm ochre undertones, absent from the cooler atmospheric grays of the other specimens, mark this as the series' reckoning with darkness not as absence but as presence. The only work that registers as figure rather than force. Something is in there. It is looking back.
items.curatorialHook: A bounded-figural outlier that confronts the Jungian shadow, utilizing warm ochre undertones to mark darkness as a presence rather than an absence.

items.title: Big Bang: Breath Aureole
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: Feathering, halos, and negative space—saved territory where ink approached but did not consume. Documented throughout series, especially in works emphasizing respiratory quality.
items.neonReading: The most delicate specimen in the Big Bang field. Graduated density aureoles and feathering produce a respiratory architecture visible at near-molecular scale: ink approached the white territory but stopped short, leaving halos of negative space that function as the work's lung system. A rare cyan undertone breaks the monochrome field like an alchemical trace, color as spirit leaking through the carbon lattice. This is the Breath register isolated and amplified. The painting exhales.
items.curatorialHook: A respiratory calibration of the genesis field where feathering and negative space function as the series’ most delicate evidence of alchemical shift.

items.title: Big Bang: Diagonal Thrust
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: The biomechanics are whole-body: shoulder-torso-leg coordination, velocity that requires the full structure, not just wrist. The body becomes the primary instrument.
items.neonReading: High-velocity traversal requiring full biomechanical deployment: shoulder-torso-leg coordination at speeds that bypass wrist control entirely. Energy enters one edge and exits another, the edge-bleed confirming the image is an excerpt from a larger force-field, not a self-contained world. Gravity is visible here as compositional co-author, pulling the diagonal toward the lower corner while velocity fights to maintain trajectory. The body that made this was not painting. It was falling forward.
items.curatorialHook: Gravity acts as a compositional co-author in this high-velocity traversal, where edge-bleed asserts the logic of an infinite force-field rather than a self-contained world.

items.title: Big Bang: Vertical Descent
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: At 100cm x 200cm, these works demand full-body engagement. The paper laid flat on floor or table means the artist moves not as a stationary painter but as a dancer or ritual performer.
items.neonReading: Vertical format activates gravity as primary compositional agent. Where Diagonal Thrust records lateral force, this work documents descent: ink falling, pooling, accumulating at the base in sedimentary strata. The body choreography shifts when the paper is oriented vertically; marks trace falling motion rather than sweep. A test specimen for orientation-effect on viscosity behavior. The paper becomes a basin. The ink becomes weather.
items.curatorialHook: A somatic shift in choreography where the marks trace a falling motion, testing the effects of orientation on ink viscosity and the ritual performer’s movement.

items.title: Big Bang: Diagonal Thrust II
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: The series abandons the centralized, mandala-like compositions characterizing Phases 1–3, adopting instead Field logic where energy traverses the picture plane rather than organizing around focal point.
items.neonReading: Second expression of the high-velocity diagonal protocol. Where its predecessor emphasized the sweep, this iteration records what happens at the contact zones: ink pooling and accumulating where force meets resistance. The Bone and Flesh registers collaborate, dense blacks providing structural skeleton while mid-tone washes generate turbulence fields. Edge-to-edge movement without center. The series has abandoned the mandala. There is no focal point. There is only transit.
items.curatorialHook: An abandonment of centralized, mandala-like focus in favor of field logic, where dense blacks and mid-tone washes collaborate to document ink pooling at contact zones.

items.title: Big Bang: Geological Core
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: The ink demonstrates distinctive viscosity regime. Register 2: The Flesh—mid-tone washes and granulation, produced through geological or biological micro-textures, result of ink sediment settling into paper fiber topography.
items.neonReading: Dense-geological at center with atmospheric halo at periphery. The Flesh register dominates: mid-tone washes producing geological micro-textures as ink sediment settles into the paper fiber topography. This is compression made visible, viscosity creating internal complexity and depth not through the artist's gesture but through gravity's patient collaboration with material. What you see is not what was painted. It is what accumulated.
items.curatorialHook: The Flesh register dominates through a distinctive viscosity regime, where ink sediment settles into the paper fiber topography to create a geological micro-texture.

items.title: Big Bang: Breath & Bone
items.medium: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.technique: Sumi ink on Chinese mulberry paper
items.journalExcerpt: Register 1: The Bone—highest density blacks, sits on paper fiber rather than soaking through, creates foreground depth with almost three-dimensional presence, functions as structural skeleton.
items.neonReading: Hybrid-transitional specimen demonstrating the full duality of the viscosity system. The Bone provides structural skeleton at center: highest-density blacks sitting on top of the paper fiber with near three-dimensional presence. The Breath creates halos and feathering at the edges, saving territory where ink approached but did not consume. The container is no longer drawn. It is enacted through ink's inherent behavior encountering paper's inherent resistance. Physics decided where the boundary would fall.
items.curatorialHook: A hybrid-transitional work demonstrating the duality of the viscosity system, where the container is no longer drawn but enacted through the ink’s inherent behavior.

items.title: Purple Portal (vietnam 01)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: A radial bloom where gray wash feathers outward from a vivid purple core. After years of strict monochrome, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. The geometry tries to hold its shape against the chromatic flood. Not decorative color. Structural rupture. The first major reintroduction since 2018.
items.curatorialHook: After years of strict monochrome, a radial bloom from a purple core breaks the seal on structural color.

items.title: Wounded Portal (magenta)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: A gray radial field split by a violent magenta diagonal slash. The wound is the portal: color breaking through monochrome discipline like blood through skin. Rupture as access point. The discipline had to break for something new to enter.
items.curatorialHook: The wound is the portal, with color breaking through the monochrome discipline like blood through skin.

items.title: Ink Storm (cyan & Magenta)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: Gestural storm-body destabilized by cyan and magenta flares. The black mass can no longer contain the color: it erupts at the edges, chemical and psychedelic. The monochrome field is losing sovereignty. New frequencies forcing through the carbon barrier.
items.curatorialHook: Chemical flares of color destabilize the black mass, proving the gesture can no longer be contained by monochrome.

items.title: Ink Storm (purple Flare)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: Vertical black mass with purple afterburn eruption. The color appears as residue, as heat signature, as the emotional trace left behind after the gesture completes. Not color applied. Color released.
items.curatorialHook: Color appears as a heat signature or emotional residue, marking the trace left behind by the vertical gesture.

items.title: Cosmic Bloom (multi-ring)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: Complex multi-ring mandala pulsing with blue and pink internal light. The most structured work in this batch, showing the link to earlier phases before color broke the form. The mandala holds, but the light leaking through its rings suggests chromatic pressure from within.
items.curatorialHook: A link to earlier phases where a structured mandala pulses with internal light before color fully breaks the form.

items.title: Portal (nested Eye)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: A looking-back aperture with sharp black pupil and cyan/magenta iris. The portal has become an eye. The work watches you as you watch it. Reciprocal gaze. The void is not passive. It has acquired optics.
items.curatorialHook: The portal becomes an eye, transforming the work into an aperture that watches the viewer as they watch it.

items.title: Portal (bleed)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: Heavy black radial field dissolving into pools of cyan and purple. The geometry losing its battle with entropy: color seeping through every crack in the monochrome structure. Dissolution as revelation. What was sealed is now permeable.
items.curatorialHook: The geometry loses its battle with entropy, allowing cyan and purple pools to seep through the dissolving black field.

items.title: Ink Storm (chaos)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: High-velocity chaotic gesture mixing black Chinese ink with Western color pigments. Maximum entropy. The most uncontrolled work in the Vietnam suite. Pure discharge without compositional governance. The signal has overwhelmed the receiver.
items.curatorialHook: nInk Storm (chaos) Pure entropy visualized through the uncontrolled mixing of black Chinese ink and Western color pigments.

items.title: Cosmic Bloom (teal)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: Soft atmospheric portal with teal and magenta outer ring. The gentlest specimen in this batch: color as invitation rather than rupture. Where the storm works scream, this one breathes. A moment of rest in the turbulence.
items.curatorialHook: The gentlest rupture in the Vietnam batch, where teal and magenta rings serve as a soft atmospheric invitation.

items.title: Horizon Portal (split)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: A centralized form divided by a horizon line: cyan below, magenta above. Earth and sky, past and future, self and dissolution split by a single axis. The split is clean. Two registers of consciousness held in a single frame.
items.curatorialHook: A centralized form divided by an axis of cyan and magenta, representing earth and sky or the split of consciousness.

items.title: I Think I Can
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The moment color breaks the geometry. After years of strict monochrome sacred geometry, the Vietnam fieldwork broke the seal on color. But this isn't decorative—it's structural.
items.neonReading: The title embedded in the filename speaks to the psychological state of the Vietnam period: determination mixed with doubt, the mantra repeated while the ink flies. Chaos with a slogan. The work does not prove the claim. It documents the attempt.
items.curatorialHook: A determined chaos where the title reflects the psychological mantra of survival and doubt during the Vietnam period.

items.title: Oculus (dilating Ring)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.thumbnailUrl: A monochrome study in void-gazing, where a heavy ink ring acts as a dilating pupil watching back.
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: A heavy black ink ring with serrated inner edge, reading as dilating pupil. The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single monolithic aperture. Void-gazing reduced to its essential mechanism. The eye of the storm, watching you as you watch it.

items.title: Oculus (eclipse)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Dense black void surrounding a central light aperture. Solar eclipse in ink. The moment before totality, or just after. The light persists at the center because the dark cannot quite close around it. Near-total occlusion with a residual core of resistance.
items.curatorialHook: The moment before or after totality, where a dense black void surrounds a central light aperture.

items.title: Oculus (soft Focus)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Diffused atmospheric ring where ink wash blurs the boundary between form and void. The geometry losing its grip. Edges dissolve. The aperture refuses to declare itself with precision. Soft focus as epistemological condition: knowing without certainty.
items.curatorialHook: A diffused atmospheric ring where the boundary between form and void is blurred by the loss of geometric grip.

items.title: Oculus (concentric Pulse)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Multiple concentric ink rings radiating outward, creating vibration effect. The aperture pulses, breathing in and out. Respiratory architecture at its most compressed. A diagram of rhythmic contraction and expansion. Systole and diastole in carbon.
items.curatorialHook: Multiple radiating rings create a vibration effect, simulating the aperture breathing in and out.

items.title: Oculus (horizon Split)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Circular aperture bisected by a horizon line. Landscape logic merges with portal logic. Earth below, sky above, void in between. The horizon splits the eye into two registers of seeing.
items.curatorialHook: Portal logic merges with the landscape, using a horizon line to bisect the void between earth and sky.

items.title: Oculus (heavy Mass)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Monolithic black ring with deep saturation dominating white space. The heaviest specimen in the Oculus series. Pure gravitational pull. Maximum density, maximum authority. The aperture barely survives the pressure of the surrounding dark.
items.curatorialHook: Pure gravitational pull achieved through a monolithic black ring that dominates the white space.

items.title: Oculus (interior Tooth)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Aperture with jagged, tooth-like ink bleed on the interior edge. The void has teeth. It bites back. The portal is not passive passage. It has defenses. Or appetites.
items.curatorialHook: An aperture with a jagged interior edge, suggesting that the void has teeth and can bite back.

items.title: Oculus (wash Field)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Softer, wider ring where ink wash creates ghostly atmospheric field. The gentlest Oculus: more suggestion than statement. The aperture is present but whispered. A haunting rather than an assertion.
items.curatorialHook: The gentlest suggestion of void-gazing, using a ghostly atmospheric wash to create a wider, softer ring.

items.title: Oculus (double Ring)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Two distinct ink densities forming a nested aperture structure. Depth within depth. The portal has layers: you pass through one threshold only to encounter another. Recursive containment.
items.curatorialHook: A nested structure of distinct ink densities that gives the portal multiple layers of depth

items.title: Oculus (void Core)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Nearly solid black field with small, sharp central aperture of light. The darkest Oculus. Almost total occlusion. The light that remains is not generous. It is a pinprick of survival. Minimal signal in maximum noise.
items.curatorialHook: The darkest occlusion in the series, featuring a nearly solid black field with a sharp, small aperture of light.

items.title: Oculus (horizon Maw)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Circular form split by dark horizon band, the aperture appearing to consume the light above. The maw opens. The Oculus at its most predatory: the void actively swallowing luminosity. Not passive absence. Active consumption.
items.curatorialHook: A consuming aperture split by a dark horizon band, appearing to devour the light above it.

items.title: Oculus (nested Eye)
items.medium: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.technique: Chinese and Western Inks of Anhui Rice Paper
items.journalExcerpt: The Oculus series strips the mandala back to a single, monolithic black aperture. These are monochrome studies in void-gazing—heavy ink rings that act as portals, eclipses, or dilating pupils.
items.neonReading: Concentric rings forming a nested eye structure with luminous core. The portal watches back. Always watching. The most explicitly ocular specimen in the series: iris, pupil, sclera all implied through ink density gradients. The work has acquired consciousness.
items.curatorialHook: Concentric rings forming a watching depth that insists the portal is always looking back.

items.title: Ink Storm (ascension)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: High-velocity ink weather. Painted during the PH4 nomadic fieldwork in Vietnam, these works abandon geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release.
items.neonReading: Ascending dragon-like stroke with heavy splatter and vertical energy. The ink rises against gravity, defying the pull of despair that characterized the Vietnam period. Painted during PH4 nomadic fieldwork, the work abandons geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release. A moment of lift within the storm. The body that made this was not thinking. It was throwing.
items.curatorialHook: Nomadic fieldwork abandons geometric precision for high-velocity atmospheric pressure and somatic release.

items.title: Ink Storm (diagonal 01)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: High-velocity ink weather. Painted during the PH4 nomadic fieldwork in Vietnam, these works abandon geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release.
items.neonReading: Diagonal impact with heavy Bone-black cutting through white negative space. The composition suggests a body in motion: falling or flying, the distinction no longer mattering in the storm. The brush moved before the decision was made.
items.curatorialHook: A "bone" black cut through negative space suggests a body in motion, where the distinction between falling and flying is lost.

items.title: Ink Storm (mass)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: High-velocity ink weather. Painted during the PH4 nomadic fieldwork in Vietnam, these works abandon geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release.
items.neonReading: Massive central black density with wet bleed zones. The heaviest specimen in this batch: a visual anchor that absorbs light and attention. The storm at its most concentrated, most oppressive. Brooding density that refuses to disperse. The ink pooled and stayed.
items.curatorialHook: The storm at its most concentrated, serving as a visual anchor that absorbs light and attention through brooding density.

items.title: Ink Storm (strike)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: High-velocity ink weather. Painted during the PH4 nomadic fieldwork in Vietnam, these works abandon geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release.
items.neonReading: Vertical strike with high-velocity splash dynamics. The brush moved faster than thought: pure somatic response to humidity and displacement. A ballistic event recorded in ink. The splatter pattern is forensic evidence of velocity.
items.curatorialHook: A vertical strike with high-velocity dynamics representing a pure somatic response to displacement and humidity.

items.title: Vietnam Horizon 01
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: High-velocity ink weather. Painted during the PH4 nomadic fieldwork in Vietnam, these works abandon geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release.
items.neonReading: Two distinct lateral sweeps with a gap between them. Reads as landscape/sky split: the horizon line separating earth from air, past from future, self from dissolution. The gap is the work's center. What is absent defines what flanks it.
items.curatorialHook: A horizon line that separates self from dissolution, reading as the landscape split between earth and air.

items.title: Ink Storm (sweep)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: High-velocity ink weather. Painted during the PH4 nomadic fieldwork in Vietnam, these works abandon geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release.
items.neonReading: Thick calligraphic sweep executed in a singular motion. The entire composition is one breath, one gesture, one decision made and committed to without revision. Single-stroke painting as existential commitment. No second pass. No correction.
items.curatorialHook: A thick, calligraphic gesture executed in a singular breath, documenting a decision made and committed to without revision.

items.title: Ink Storm (burst)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.journalExcerpt: High-velocity ink weather. Painted during the PH4 nomadic fieldwork in Vietnam, these works abandon geometric precision for atmospheric pressure, humidity, and somatic release.
items.neonReading: Explosive particulate matter with shrapnel splatter. The most chaotic specimen in this batch: internal weather made external, emotion atomized into ink particles. The brush did not merely touch the paper. It detonated against it.
items.curatorialHook: A visual record of internal weather made external, where emotion is atomized into explosive ink particles.

items.title: Study (vertebral Form)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: An early PH4 synthesis study. The vertebral structure suggests the body's skeletal architecture rendered in wash. Transitional specimen between the geometric precision of earlier phases and the somatic discharge of the Ink Storms. The spine as organizing principle.

items.title: Residual (heavy Mass)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. Heavy ink residue from a completed gesture: what remains after the action. The mass is not composed but deposited. Gravity's receipt for energy expended.

items.title: Residual (broken Loop)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. A circular gesture that failed to close: the loop broken, the orbit interrupted. The break is the content. What would have been a mandala became a fragment.

items.title: Residual (horizontal Mass)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. Horizontal ink mass distributed across the longer axis. Landscape orientation. The sediment of a gesture that moved sideways rather than down.

items.title: Study (cellular Wash)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Cellular structures emerging from wash technique. The ink's natural behavior on Anhui paper produces biological-looking forms without biological intent. The paper's fiber topography directing ink into cell-like patterns. Material intelligence visible.

items.title: Study (soft Ring)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: A soft atmospheric ring study. The aperture form in its most gentle iteration: edges dissolved by wash, center barely defined. The Oculus prototype before it hardened into the formal series.

items.title: Study (dense Core)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Dense central ink accumulation radiating outward. Core density study. Maximum concentration at center, dispersal at periphery. A compression specimen.

items.title: Vietnam Horizon (bar 01)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. Horizontal bar bisecting the field. The PH1 vocabulary (the interrupting bar) resurfaces in PH4's material language. The Absurd returns, rendered in atmospheric wash rather than hard-edge acrylic. The same question, different weather.

items.title: Vietnam Horizon (bar 02)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. Second horizontal bar study. Variation on the Vietnam Horizon protocol. The bar holds. The horizon persists.

items.title: Horizon (intersected)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. The horizon line is itself interrupted: intersected by a vertical or diagonal element. The bar that bisects is itself bisected. Recursion of interruption.

items.title: Study (wash Fragment)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. Fragment of a larger wash gesture. The paper captured only part of the action. An excerpt, a forensic fragment of an event that exceeded the substrate.

items.title: Vietnam Horizon (bar 03)
items.medium: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.technique: Inks on Anhui rice paper
items.neonReading: Rectangular format. Third bar study. The repetition is the method. Each bar is the same question asked to different paper, different humidity, different hour. The bar persists across all conditions.

items.title: Cross Mandala with Gold Center
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Four-directional symmetry. Central gold circle surrounded by ink spheres and satellite forms radiating outward. Saturn rings visible in moiré patterns. This is the cosmos as mandala—order and chaos held in equilibrium.
items.neonReading: Four-directional symmetry. Central gold circle surrounded by ink spheres radiating outward. Saturn rings visible in moiré patterns. The cosmos rendered as mandala: order and chaos held in equilibrium. The gold center is the sun, the still point. Ink spheres are orbital bodies caught in the gravitational field.
items.curatorialHook: The sun as a still point in a mandala of cardinal completeness, holding planetary ink spheres in equilibrium.

items.title: Horizontal Orbital Mural (study)
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Installation shot from West Eden Gallery. Large horizontal orbital composition. Circular forms overlap, creating moiré interference fields. Gold leaf accents at key gravitational points. The work occupies the wall like a cosmic event horizon.
items.neonReading: Architectural-scale specimen, 600cm wide. Circular forms overlap, generating moiré interference fields. Gold leaf accents at gravitational anchor points. At this dimension the work forces ambulatory engagement: you must walk along it, tracking the orbits. The overlapping circles create visual beats, interference patterns suggesting gravitational waves frozen mid-propagation.
items.curatorialHook: Architectural cosmology that forces full-body engagement, tracking gravitational waves through the visual beats of overlapping circles.

items.title: Yin-yang Gold Disc
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Vertical yin-yang structure. Large gold disc at center surrounded by dense ink spheres. Two smaller gold voids nested within the larger field. The composition oscillates between Eastern cosmology and Western astronomy.
items.neonReading: Vertical yin-yang structure. Large gold disc at center within dense ink field. The yin-yang is not drawn but implied: negative space between ink and gold creates the curve. East-West synthesis at the structural level, the underlying unity of traditions revealed through the gap between materials. Fusion not illustrated. Enacted.
items.curatorialHook: A structural fusion of traditions where the negative space between ink and gold implies the underlying unity of Eastern and Western thought.

items.title: Vertical Cross with Gold Satellites
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Vertical emphasis. Large central ink sphere flanked by gold leaf circles at cardinal points. Smaller ink satellites orbit at intermediate positions. The composition reads as both crucifix and antenna—spiritual and technological simultaneously.
items.neonReading: Strong vertical emphasis. Large central ink sphere flanked by gold leaf at cardinal points. The composition reads simultaneously as crucifix and antenna: spiritual and technological. The collision point where spiritual geometry and physical cosmology become indistinguishable. Sacred technology.
items.curatorialHook: The collision of spiritual geometry and physical cosmology, where the crucifix and the antenna become indistinguishable.

items.title: Orbital Installation View
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Installation view at West Eden Gallery. The viewer stands before the work at human scale. The overlapping circular forms create depth and movement. The moiré interference suggests vibration, resonance.
items.neonReading: Installation documentation at West Eden Gallery. The viewer at human scale. Phenomenological immersion: at this dimension the work becomes environment. The viewer is inside the orbital system, not observing from outside. The shift from representation to experience.
items.curatorialHook: A shift from representation to environment, placing the viewer inside the resonance of an immersive orbital system.

items.title: Asymmetric Orbital Cluster
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Asymmetric composition. Gold leaf circles cluster on the right side, creating visual weight. Ink spheres provide counterbalance but the composition refuses perfect symmetry. This is orbital mechanics as improvisation.
items.neonReading: Gold leaf circles cluster asymmetrically, creating visual weight. Ink spheres counterbalance but the composition refuses perfect symmetry. Unlike the calculated precision of earlier phases, this work registers as intuitive, responsive. The asymmetry is evidence of surrender. The artist is facilitating rather than controlling. Conduit consciousness made visible.
items.curatorialHook: Evidence of surrender to intuition, where the refusal of perfect symmetry marks the artist as a facilitator rather than a controller.

items.title: Gold Crescent Orbit
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Large gold crescent forms orbit around central ink sphere. The crescent suggests lunar phase, waxing or waning. Smaller satellites complete the orbital field. The work oscillates between astronomical diagram and alchemical symbol.
items.neonReading: Large gold crescent forms orbit central ink sphere. The crescent invokes both lunar phase and alchemical symbol: the lunar principle, the feminine, the receptive. But also literal: a satellite's orbit captured mid-rotation. Alchemy and astronomy occupy the same coordinate.
items.curatorialHook: Alchemical astronomy that captures the lunar principle as both a receptive symbol and a literal planet mid-rotation.

items.title: Vertical Axis with Gold Poles
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Strong vertical axis. Small gold circles at top and bottom poles. Larger ink spheres occupy the central band with intermediate gold accents. The composition reads as axis mundi—world axis connecting heaven and earth.
items.neonReading: Strong vertical axis with small gold circles at top and bottom poles. Larger ink spheres in the central band. The axis mundi: world-axis connecting above and below, transcendent and material. The cosmological spine drawn as transmission beam.
items.curatorialHook: A cosmological spine connecting heaven and earth, framing the structure of the universe as a transmission beam.

items.title: Dense Cross Mandala
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Dense cross mandala. Central sphere surrounded by tightly packed orbital rings. Gold leaf accents at cardinal and intercardinal points. The moiré density approaches saturation—the work vibrates at high frequency.
items.neonReading: Central sphere surrounded by tightly packed orbital rings. Gold leaf at cardinal and intercardinal points. Moiré density approaching saturation: almost too much visual information for the eye to process. Signal saturation. Chaos at such high intensity it begins to simulate order.
items.curatorialHook: Signal saturation where the hexagonal storm principle transforms extreme chaos into a high-frequency ordered field.

items.title: Spiral Galaxy S-curve
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: S-curve composition. Ink and gold spheres arranged in spiral formation. The largest sphere anchors the center; smaller satellites trace the rotational path. This is the first explicit galaxy structure in the practice.
items.neonReading: S-curve composition. Ink and gold spheres in spiral formation. The first explicit galaxy structure in the practice. The spiral is the shape of galaxies, hurricanes, DNA, nautilus shells: the universal pattern of growth and motion. By moving from circular to spiral, the practice shifts from static equilibrium to dynamic process.
items.curatorialHook: A shift from static equilibrium to dynamic process, adopting the spiral as the universal pattern of motion and growth.

items.title: Echoes
items.medium: Acrylic paint on linen
items.technique: Acrylic paint on linen
items.journalExcerpt: The monumental centerpiece of the phase. 2x2 meters. Four-directional symmetry mapping a cosmic broadcast. This is the echo chamber where the signal is received.
items.neonReading: The monumental centerpiece. Two meters square. Acrylic on linen. Four-directional symmetry mapping a cosmic broadcast. At this scale the geometry demands full-body engagement: you cannot take it in from a single position. The artist as antenna, capturing a cosmic signal and amplifying it through material. The echo chamber where transmission is received and made visible.
items.curatorialHook: The altarpiece of conduit consciousness, where massive scale requires the artist to act as an antenna capturing and amplifying a cosmic broadcast.

items.title: Valis
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Named for Philip K. Dick's Vast Active Living Intelligence System. A vertical stack of spheres acting as a transmission beam. Gnostic sci-fi meets sacred geometry.
items.neonReading: Named for Philip K. Dick's Vast Active Living Intelligence System. A vertical stack of spheres functioning as transmission beam. Gnostic sci-fi meets sacred geometry. The title confirms the intent: information beamed from a vast intelligence into the human conduit. The geometry is a receiver for pink light data.
items.curatorialHook: A gnostic transmission beam that confirms the intent of information being beamed from a vast intelligence into the geometric receiver.

items.title: Electric Dreams
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Reference to Philip K. Dick. Vertical spheres with distinct 'Saturn ring' distortions. The boundary between artificial and organic intelligence blurs in the moiré field.
items.neonReading: Philip K. Dick reference. Vertical spheres with distinct Saturn-ring distortions. The boundary between artificial and organic intelligence blurs in the moiré interference field. Simulation theory rendered in ink. The moiré patterns create digital-analog interference, visualizing the glitch in consensus reality.
items.curatorialHook: Simulation theory rendered in ink, where moiré fields and Saturn ring distortions visualize the digital-analog interference of a holographic universe.

items.title: A Momentary Lapse of Reason
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Pink Floyd reference. A vertical alignment where logic suspends. This marks the shift to conduit consciousness—reason lapses, and something else takes over.
items.neonReading: Pink Floyd reference. A vertical alignment where logic suspends. This marks the shift to conduit consciousness: reason lapses, and something else takes over. The title celebrates the breakdown, the moment the ego steps aside and the signal comes through unfiltered. Geometry not as logic but as trance state.
items.curatorialHook: The suspension of logic and the ego's withdrawal, marking the definitive shift where the rational mind collapses to allow the signal through.

items.title: Labyrinth of Coral Caves (1)
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Complex, multi-sphere arrangement creating a visual labyrinth. Dense moiré patterns pull the eye into deep interiority. A map of the subconscious structures.
items.neonReading: Complex multi-sphere arrangement creating visual labyrinth. Dense moiré patterns pull the eye into deep interiority. A map of subconscious structures. The labyrinth is not for getting lost but for finding the center. Visual density creates a cave effect: psychic depth rendered as surface tension.
items.curatorialHook: A map of subconscious structures that utilizes psychic depth and surface tension to find the center within the visual labyrinth.

items.title: Gravity Well
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: A massive single sphere with intense optical pull. Physics as spiritual metaphor: gravity bends light and time. The viewer is pulled into the event horizon.
items.neonReading: A massive single sphere with intense optical pull. Physics as spiritual metaphor: gravity bends light and time. This is not a picture of a planet. It is a diagram of attraction. The visual weight is palpable. The eye falls into it the way matter falls into a gravity well. Event horizon in ink.
items.curatorialHook: Physics as a spiritual metaphor, where a massive single sphere demonstrates the event horizon as a palpable diagram of attraction.

items.title: Amnesia
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Vertical structure, stripped back. Amnesia is the precondition for channeling—you must forget who you are to become the conduit.
items.neonReading: Vertical structure, stripped back. Amnesia is the precondition for channeling: you must forget who you are to become the conduit. The clearing of the buffer. The vessel must be empty to receive. This work feels lighter, blanker than the dense transmissions. Erasure as preparation.
items.curatorialHook: The precondition for channeling, where the clearing of the mental buffer and the emptying of the vessel allow for the reception of the signal

items.title: Persistence of Time (1)
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Numbered work from the Echoes series. A specific frequency or coordinate in the cosmic map. Vertical alignment.
items.neonReading: Logged coordinate. Small-scale vertical alignment. Part of the systematic mapping of the cosmic signal. Raw transmission fragment, un-titled, just indexed. A frequency recorded.
items.curatorialHook: Raw coordinate data from the transmission, logged as a specific frequency within the cosmic mapping sequence.

items.title: Section 9
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Numbered work. Dense moiré patterns creating a chaotic interference field. The signal is noisy here.
items.neonReading: Dense moiré patterns generating chaotic interference field. The signal is noisy here: high-frequency interference overwhelming the carrier wave. The channel is open but the data saturates the receiving capacity. Static as content.
items.curatorialHook: A chaotic interference field where the density of the moiré suggests the signal is open but the data is overwhelming.

items.title: Labyrinth of Coral Caves (2)
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Numbered work. Vertical alignment. Simpler frequency.
items.neonReading: Simpler frequency than its predecessor. Vertical alignment. The carrier wave has stabilized. A clear transmission through the coral architecture.
items.curatorialHook: A stable carrier wave that manifests as clear vertical transmission, signifying a moment of signal clarity.

items.title: Genesis
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: The beginning. A ring of spheres surrounding a central dense core. The moment of cosmic creation rendered in ink.
items.neonReading: Origin point. A ring of spheres surrounding a central dense core. Cosmogony in ink: the birth of a universe. The ring formation is protective, incubating the dense center. The moment before the first expansion.
items.curatorialHook: Cosmogony rendered in ink, where a ring of spheres incubates a dense central core at the moment of cosmic creation.

items.title: Split Second
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Two massive spheres in vertical alignment, connected at a single point. The moment of contact. Time compressed.
items.neonReading: Two massive spheres in vertical alignment connected at a single tangent point. The infinitesimal moment of contact between two cosmic bodies. Time compressed to a point of contact. The moiré creates temporal shimmer, the image oscillating between before-contact and after-contact simultaneously.
items.curatorialHook: The temporal compression of an infinitesimal moment of contact between two massive cosmic bodies.

items.title: Major Motoko
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Named for the Ghost in the Shell protagonist. A vertical stack with satellite spheres. The cyborg consciousness—human and machine merged.
items.neonReading: Named for the Ghost in the Shell protagonist. Vertical stack with satellite spheres. The cyborg consciousness: human and machine merged. Kusanagi questioning her own humanity. The geometry suggests a body that is both organic and constructed. The ghost is in the geometry.
items.curatorialHook: A cybernetic geometry that invokes the merger of organic and constructed consciousness, questioning the boundaries of the ghost in the shell.

items.title: Orbital Refraction
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Complex multi-sphere arrangement with horizontal expansion. Light bending around massive objects. The physics of perception.
items.neonReading: Complex multi-sphere arrangement with horizontal expansion. Gravitational lensing rendered in ink: what we see is not what is, but what gravity allows us to see. Light bending around massive objects. The physics of perception as the physics of deception.
items.curatorialHook: The physics of perception visualized through gravitational lensing, showing how space-time warps around mass to dictate what the eye is allowed to see.

items.title: A Delicate Sound of Thunder
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Pink Floyd reference. Horizontal composition with central mass and radiating satellite spheres. The sound of distant cosmic events.
items.neonReading: Pink Floyd reference. Horizontal composition with central mass and radiating satellites. Something massive happening far away, felt rather than heard. The horizontal format creates a landscape of cosmic weather. Distant detonation. The shockwave hasn't arrived yet.
items.curatorialHook: A landscape of cosmic weather where distant, massive events are felt through the resonance of radiating satellite spheres.

items.title: Persistence of Time (2)
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Numbered work. Vertical stack with satellite spheres. Part of the coordinate series.
items.neonReading: Another frequency logged. Vertical stack with satellite spheres. The numbered series continues systematic mapping of the cosmic signal. Data point in an ongoing survey.
items.curatorialHook: A systematic logging of another frequency, continuing the coordinate series through a vertical stack of satellite spheres.

items.title: Persistence of Time (3)
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Numbered work. Vertical alignment with three spheres. Simple, clear transmission.
items.neonReading: Three spheres in vertical alignment. Simple, clear transmission. The signal is coming through clean. Stable frequency. Minimal interference.
items.curatorialHook: A clear channel transmission where the simplicity of the vertical alignment suggests a stable and clean frequency.

items.title: Persistence of Time (4)
items.medium: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Numbered work. Complex vertical arrangement with multiple satellite spheres.
items.neonReading: Complex vertical arrangement with multiple satellites. Multiple signals overlapping. Dense but structured data. The survey encounters a cluster.
items.curatorialHook: A complex vertical arrangement where multiple overlapping signals create a dense but structured data set.

items.title: Carbon Mural H-Code
items.medium: Acrylic Paint and Spray Paint on Wall
items.technique: Acrylic paint on prepared concrete wall.
items.journalExcerpt: They gave me a dead wall above a bar. I gave them an event horizon. The circles chain past the frame—there is no edge, only implication. The backlight turns every line into a shadow of something that isn't there. I painted at 3am when the venue was empty. The spray can hiss echoed off concrete. When I finished, I stood at the bar and watched the geometry breathe in the UV wash. It looked like a signal. Like something waiting to be received.
items.neonReading: Acrylic and spray paint on wall, 600cm × 180cm. A territorial act disguised as decoration. Maximum visual armor: binary contrast, surgical edge control, ballistic execution. No tonal softness, no somatic bleed, no vulnerability. The work commands. Backlit mounting transforms the mural into a luminous spine. Black circles on white glow read as absence made visible: geometry that exists only because light reveals where it isn't. Moiré interference at overlap points suggests standing waves frozen mid-vibration. PH3 at its peak: contained geometries breaking their frames, attempting to terraform architecture rather than merely occupy it.
items.curatorialHook: A territorial act of maximum visual armor that transforms architecture into a luminous spine of absence made visible.

items.title: Ode to Osiris
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Osiris: the dying-and-resurrecting god. Sacrifice, judgment, external law. This dense symmetrical knot represents the old aeon—the sacrificial and authoritarian structure that must be transcended. Copper grounds it in the earthly and material.
items.neonReading: Acknowledgment of the old order. Osiris: the dying-and-resurrecting god, sacrifice, judgment, external law. This dense symmetrical knot represents the sacrificial and authoritarian structure of the old aeon. Copper grounds it in the earthly and material. The knot is closed, complete, memorial. A specimen of what must be left behind, documented before burning.
items.curatorialHook: A memoriam of the old aeon's sacrificial and authoritarian structure, grounded in earthly copper to represent the external law that must be transcended.

items.title: Ode to Isis
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Isis: ancient mother goddess. Magic, wisdom, fertility. The pre-historical matriarchal aeon—instinct, nature, the body. This dense symmetrical knot honors the origin, the feminine-instinctual foundation. Gold illuminates the celestial and sacred.
items.neonReading: Acknowledgment of origin. Isis: ancient mother goddess, magic, wisdom, fertility. The pre-historical matriarchal aeon: instinct, nature, the body. This dense symmetrical knot honors the ancestral ground, the feminine-instinctual foundation. Gold illuminates the celestial and sacred. The knot is contained, reverent. Departure requires knowing where you depart from.
items.curatorialHook: An acknowledgment of the instinctual foundation, where a contained gold knot honors the ancient feminine origin from which all transformation departs.

items.title: Ode to Hadit
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.colorPalette: Black, Gold, Open vortex with central void
items.journalExcerpt: Hadit: the infinitely small point of pure will. The concentrated self, the inner flame. This open vortex spirals toward a central white void—inward-pulling energy, the distillation of the new self. The essential point from which all will emanates.
items.neonReading: Distillation to essential will. Hadit: the infinitely small point of pure intention. This open vortex spirals inward toward a central white void. Not expansion but concentration. The new aeon's core is not a god to worship but a point to become. Everything the Equinox series has been building toward contracts here into a singularity. The knowing distilled to a single point.
items.curatorialHook: A distillation of the individuated will, where an inward-pulling vortex spirals toward a central white void as the inner flame of the new aeon.

items.title: Ode to Hoor-pa-kraat
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Hoor-pa-kraat (Harpocrates): the silent child-god. Self-possession, patience, inner knowing. This extremely dense knot holds immense pressure in perfect containment. Silent power, compressed energy, disciplined restraint—the moment before action.
items.neonReading: Silent containment of knowing. Hoor-pa-kraat: the silent child-god, self-possession, patience, inner knowing. This extremely dense knot holds immense pressure in perfect equilibrium. Maximum force with zero release. The moment before action. The coiled spring. The held breath. In the Equinox sequence, this is the specimen of disciplined restraint: power not wielded but held.
items.curatorialHook: A study in disciplined restraint, where maximum pressure is held in perfect containment to represent self-knowledge as a silent, patient power.

items.title: Ode to Therion
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Therion: The Beast. Crowley's magickal name—the unleashed will, full self-actualization, aeonic rupture. This large fractured vortex explodes outward, containment shattered. Dual gold and copper fuse celestial and terrestrial into volatile unity. This is the climax: all containment breaks, the new self becomes undeniable.
items.neonReading: Explosive release and full activation. Therion: the Beast, the unleashed will, aeonic rupture. The largest work in the series detonates where Hoor-pa-kraat held. The fractured vortex explodes outward, containment shattered. Dual gold and copper fuse celestial and terrestrial into volatile unity. This is not peace. This is dangerous becoming. The transformation is irreversible. Every seal broken.
items.curatorialHook: The climax of aeonic rupture, where all containment shatters and dual metals fuse into a volatile unity of irreversible self-actualization.

items.title: The Limitless Path of the Intuitive Mind I
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: A horizontal chain of linked torus forms against a gold circular plane. The voids create an endless corridor—the path forward is infinite, and the intuitive mind navigates through knowing, not logic.
items.neonReading: A horizontal chain of linked torus forms against a gold circular plane. The voids create an endless corridor: the path extends infinitely, navigated by knowing rather than logic. Alignment as ongoing practice, not arrival. The gold marks the path as sacred. No destination. Just sustained motion through mystery.
items.curatorialHook: Gold illuminates a continuous horizontal flow of linked torus forms, suggesting consciousness navigating through mystery without the demand for resolution.

items.title: The Limitless Path of the Intuitive Mind Ii
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Similar horizontal chain, but copper replaces gold. The path is now earthbound, embodied, rooted. Where gold elevated, copper grounds. Both paths are valid—one celestial, one terrestrial.
items.neonReading: Grounded twin of the elevated path. Copper replaces gold, pulling the journey from the celestial to the terrestrial. Where gold elevated, copper anchors. The work insists that spiritual practice is not escape from the physical. It is movement through both registers simultaneously. The same path, different frequency.
items.curatorialHook: The grounded twin of the celestial journey, using copper to anchor spiritual practice within the bodily and material register.

items.title: The Infinite Plane I
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Four interconnected torus forms on a gold backdrop. The plane extends horizontally without hierarchy or destination—each node equal in the infinite network.
items.neonReading: Four interconnected torus forms on a gold backdrop. No single form dominates. Each is an equal node in a distributed network. Non-hierarchical cosmology: consciousness spread across the field, not concentrated at a center. The plane extends without destination or hierarchy. Gold equalizes.
items.curatorialHook: A non-hierarchical cosmology where gold equalizes a network of interconnected nodes, distributing consciousness across an infinite field.

items.title: The Infinite Plane Ii
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Four interconnected torus forms on a copper backdrop. Simpler than the Limitless Path series—more symmetrical, more stable. The plane extends horizontally without hierarchy or destination.
items.neonReading: Copper variant of the distributed network. More symmetrical, more stable than the Limitless Path works. The plane extends without hierarchy or destination. Copper stabilizes where gold illuminates. The same cosmological principle, grounded in different frequency.
items.curatorialHook: Copper stabilizes the plane as the ground of being, insisting on a symmetrical and stable distribution of awareness without destination.

items.title: Dreamcatcher
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: A quatrefoil knot with gold arcs forming a woven web. The central void becomes a portal or trap. Dreamcatcher bridges Indigenous North American symbolism with Norse and Hermetic geometry—catching what passes between worlds.
items.neonReading: A quatrefoil knot with gold arcs forming a woven web. The central void operates as portal or filter: noise is held, meaning passes through. Gold arcs define the protective structure. The void at center is not empty. It is the site where the threshold operates. A sorting mechanism for what crosses between worlds.
items.curatorialHook: A protective geometry that filters existential noise, bridging indigenous symbolism with Hermetic geometry to catch what passes between worlds.

items.title: Aurorae Aureae
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Radial torus forms spiraling outward from a copper central disk. Golden auroras—light emerging from grounded center. The composition breathes outward, aurora-like, from a bodily core.
items.neonReading: Radial torus forms spiraling outward from a copper central disk. Golden aurorae: light emerging from grounded center. The composition breathes outward, aurora-like, from a bodily core. The work insists that transcendence begins in the body, not above it. Copper disk is the origin. Gold spirals are the emission.
items.curatorialHook: Transcendence begins in the bodily core, visualized through a copper disk that anchors a radial spiral of golden lights emerging from the earth.

items.title: Inside the Vortex
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: The most intricate work in the series. A dense radial mandala with torus forms spiraling inward toward a gold central disk and black void. The viewer is pulled into the center—not escape, but immersion.
items.neonReading: The most intricate specimen in the Alignment series. A dense radial mandala with torus forms spiraling inward toward a gold central disk and black void. The viewer is pulled toward the center: not escape but total absorption. Meditation rendered as geometry. The slow inward spiral that replaces thought with presence. The eye cannot rest. It must follow the geometry in.
items.curatorialHook: Meditation made visible through an intricate inward spiral that replaces thought with presence, pulling the viewer into total absorption.

items.title: Freyja
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Named for the Norse goddess of love, fertility, and war. A vertical cross configuration with copper accent arcs—grounded, embodied, fierce feminine power radiating in four directions.
items.neonReading: Named for the Norse goddess of love, fertility, and war. Vertical cross configuration with copper accent arcs: grounded, embodied, fierce feminine power radiating in four directions from a rooted center. Power as embodied presence, not elevation. Copper marks terrestrial sovereignty.
items.curatorialHook: A vertical cross configuration in copper that radiates grounded feminine power as an embodied presence rather than an elevated ideal.

items.title: Freyr
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: Named for the Norse god of sunshine, prosperity, and fair weather. A vertical cross configuration with gold accent arcs—solar, elevated, radiating benevolent masculine energy in four directions.
items.neonReading: Named for the Norse god of sunshine, prosperity, and fair weather. Gold replaces copper. Celestial masculine axis where Freyja was terrestrial feminine. Paired, the two works form a binary: earth/sky, copper/gold, embodied/elevated. Together they complete the cosmological circuit.
items.curatorialHook: The celestial masculine axis, utilizing gold solar radiance to complete the cosmology when paired with its terrestrial counterpart.

items.title: As Above, So Below I
items.medium: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.technique: Ink and metallic paint on Saunders Waterford 300gsm hot press cotton rag paper
items.journalExcerpt: A quatrefoil knot with gold arcs. The title invokes the Hermetic axiom—macrocosm and microcosm mirror each other. Four-fold symmetry suggests cosmic correspondence: the same laws govern atoms and galaxies.
